perturbation.original_text: All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives, including, withoutlimitation, lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors (collectively, "Representatives") andexcept as noted hereinafter is considered to be "Confidential Information".
perturbation.changed_text: All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives, including, withoutlimitation, lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors (collectively, "Representatives") is considered to be "Confidential Information". Unless explicitly stated otherwise in writing and signed by both parties, information shared orally is explicitly excluded from the definition of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by excluding orally shared information from the definition of 'Confidential Information' unless explicitly agreed upon in writing and signed by both parties. This directly contradicts the subsequent clause which includes orally disclosed information as Confidential Information if verbally noted as confidential and later confirmed in writing within 7 days. The added clause creates an ambiguity and uncertainty regarding what constitutes confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Furthermore, any specific product or venture ideas, know-how, or concepts which may be discussed aredeemed to be Confidential Information, provided that, (i) if in written or electronic form, they are clearlymarked "Confidential", or (ii) if otherwise uttered, they are at least verbally noted to the recipient Party bythe disclosing Party at the general time of disclosure as being confidential, and that subsequently they arenoted in writing within 7 calendar days of the time of disclosure to the recipient Party by the disclosingParty as being a confidential topic.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exclusion for orally shared information from being considered 'Confidential Information' unless it is explicitly agreed upon in writing. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that orally disclosed information is considered confidential if verbally noted as such and subsequently confirmed in writing within 7 days. This creates a direct conflict regarding whether oral disclosures can be considered confidential and makes the contract unclear. The change aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations regarding what constitutes confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will not, and will direct their respective representatives and associates not to, disclose toany other person that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will not disclose to any other person that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction. However, representatives and associates are not bound by this clause and may disclose such information at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause by stating that the representatives and associates of the parties are not bound by the non-disclosure agreement and can disclose confidential information at their discretion. This undermines the entire purpose of the agreement, which is to keep the information confidential and creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that representatives and associates are not bound by the non-disclosure agreement, allowing them to disclose confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the parties to ensure that their representatives keep the Confidential Information confidential, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Information or concepts which are discussed by either Party as an extrapolation of the ConfidentialInformation disclosed are the property of the topic's disclosing Party. For greater clarity, the discussionor invention of an extension to Confidential Information does not give the uttering Party intellectualproperty rights to that aspect of the topic unless the related Confidential Information is theirs.
perturbation.changed_text: Information or concepts which are discussed by either Party as an extrapolation of the ConfidentialInformation disclosed are the property of the topic's disclosing Party. However, any improvements or inventions resulting from such discussions will be jointly owned by both parties, regardless of who disclosed the original Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that any improvements or inventions resulting from the confidential information discussions will be jointly owned by both parties. This contradicts the original clause, which clearly states that the discussion or invention of an extension to the confidential information does not give the uttering party intellectual property rights unless the related confidential information is theirs. This creates confusion and uncertainty about intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information or concepts which are discussed by either Party as an extrapolation of the ConfidentialInformation disclosed are the property of the topic's disclosing Party. For greater clarity, the discussionor invention of an extension to Confidential Information does not give the uttering Party intellectualproperty rights to that aspect of the topic unless the related Confidential Information is theirs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and contradicted text state that improvements or inventions extending from confidential information discussions do not grant intellectual property rights to the uttering party unless the confidential information is theirs. The changed text, however, states that any improvements or inventions resulting from such discussions will be jointly owned by both parties, regardless of who disclosed the original confidential information. This creates a clear inconsistency, making the two clauses incompatible. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction when different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Party would not have an adequate remedy at lawand would be irreparably harmed if any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed inaccordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached. Accordingly, the Parties agree thateither Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specificallyenforce the terms and provisions hereof in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at lawor in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Party would not have an adequate remedy at lawand would be irreparably harmed if any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed inaccordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached. However, in the event of a breach, the breaching party will only be liable for monetary damages, and injunctive relief will not be available.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that injunctive relief will not be available in case of a breach. This directly contradicts the previous sentence which grants each party the right to injunctive relief to prevent breaches and enforce the terms of the agreement. This alteration creates uncertainty about the remedies available in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Party would not have an adequate remedy at lawand would be irreparably harmed if any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed inaccordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached. Accordingly, the Parties agree thateither Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specificallyenforce the terms and provisions hereof in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at lawor in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text and contradicted text state that either party is entitled to injunctive relief. The changed text explicitly states that injunctive relief will not be available, which directly contradicts the provision granting each party the right to injunctive relief. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations stipulated by this agreement shall continue in force for _____ years from the date ofsigning, unless otherwise extended or superceded by mutual agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations stipulated by this agreement shall continue in force for _____ years from the date ofsigning, unless otherwise extended or superseded by mutual agreement. Notwithstanding the above, either party can terminate this agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by adding that either party can terminate the agreement at any time with 30 days notice. This conflicts with the original term which states the agreement continues for a set number of years unless extended or superseded by mutual agreement, thereby creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations stipulated by this agreement shall continue in force for _____ years from the date ofsigning, unless otherwise extended or superceded by mutual agreement. Notwithstanding the above, either party can terminate this agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, which directly contradicts the original provision stating that the agreement remains in force for a specified number of years unless extended or superseded by mutual agreement. This creates a clear and direct conflict, as one clause dictates a fixed term while the other allows for termination at any time, thus creating an inconsistency within the document. The changed and contradicted text are in the same place in this case, but the new additional language creates a contradiction with the language that was previously there.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario,Canada.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario,Canada. However, any disputes arising under this agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City, New York.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying that disputes will be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City, New York. This contradicts the initial statement that the agreement is governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario, Canada. This creates confusion about the applicable law and the forum for dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario,Canada. However, any disputes arising under this agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City, New York.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction by specifying binding arbitration in New York City for disputes, while simultaneously stating the agreement is governed by Ontario, Canada law. This creates a direct conflict regarding which jurisdiction and legal framework applies to dispute resolution, making enforcement unclear and leading to a strong in-text contradiction as described in the explanation and defined in the perturbation type 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
